steps.title: Separate average from extremes
steps.content: The meta-analysis found larger differences for fish studies on average than for bird studies. But every animal class had individual studies with differences well above the average for fish.

steps.title: Reject unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice A discusses whether effects were labeled detrimental, which the passage does not address. Choice B contradicts the fish average. Choice C makes an unsupported claim about one common carp study.

steps.title: Infer overlap between studies
steps.content: If some bird studies were well above the fish average, then some bird studies must have shown larger effects than at least some fish studies. The answer is D.
